sections.paragraph:
f.-

(1) bitterness, Deu 32:32, אַשְׁכְּלֹות מְרֹרֹות “clusters of bitternesses,” i.e. bitter. Metaph. bitter, severe (punishments), Job 13:26, תִכְתֹּב עָלַי מְרֹרֹות כִּי “that thou writest (such) bitter things upon me,” thou layest on me such heavy punishment.

(2) bile, gall, Job 20:25. מְרֹורַת פְּתָנִים “the gall of vipers,” ib. 14, used of the poison of vipers, which the ancients incorrectly supposed to be in the gall (Plin. H. N. xi. 37, § 62), although in other forms also of this root, the notion of bitterness is applied to venom; (see מְרִירִי , Syr. ܡܶܪܬܐܳ Zab. מררא venom).
